title: add JLCPCB footprint autocomplete
description: Adds a generator for JLCPCB footprint autocomplete using the public jlcsearch API, enabling type-safe autocomplete for jlcpcb: footprint references in footprint props and pcbSx selectors.
body:
## Summary
- add a generator for `lib/generated/jlcpcb-autocomplete.ts` using the public jlcsearch API
- export the generated JLCPCB autocomplete path type and include it in `FootprintProp`
- allow `jlcpcb:` footprint selectors in `pcbSx` and update the footprint autocomplete type test
- add the package script needed to regenerate the JLCPCB autocomplete dataset
- keep the generator output Biome-clean so the generated file passes formatting checks

## Why
JLCPCB footprint source strings were missing from the typed autocomplete surface, so consumers could not get type-safe autocomplete for `jlcpcb:` footprint references.

## Impact
Consumers can now use `jlcpcb:C...` footprint references with autocomplete and type support across footprint props and the matching `pcbSx` selector path.

## Validation
- `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-jlcpcb-autocomplete.ts`
- `bun test`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run format:check`

title: add nominalTraceWidth field to Net
description: Adds a nominalTraceWidth field to the Net interface to specify the width of traces in the circuit.

title: Handle CircleSchema silkscreen circles in conversion
description: Summary convert EasyEDA CIRCLE shapes on silkscreen layers into pcb_silkscreen_path output approximate silkscreen circles with 24 segments so they render in soup and generated TypeScript footprints update affected conversion tests and snapshots to cover the newly emitted silkscreen geometry  Why Silkscreen circles were being dropped during conversion. That meant circular outline and marker graphics present in EasyEDA footprints did not make it into the generated soupTypeScript output or snapshots.  Impact Generated footprints now preserve circular silkscreen details more faithfully, improving visual accuracy for downstream rendering and snapshot-based regression coverage.  Root Cause The converter handled paths and arcs for silkscreen output, but it did not have a CIRCLE branch for silkscreen-layer shapes.  Validation bun test --timeout 20000 testsconvert-to-soup-testsC265111.test.ts testsconvert-to-soup-testsc3178291-to-soup.test.ts testsconvert-to-soup-testsc46497.test.ts testsconvert-to-soup-testsc88224.test.ts testsconvert-to-soup-testsesp32-to-soup.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC113367-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC12084-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC128415-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC19076967-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC2040-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC23689428-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC265111-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC2652953-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC2838502-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC2848306-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC2913206-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC2998002-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC3178291-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC393941-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC46497-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC51950748-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC7203002-to-ts.test.ts testsconvert-to-tsC88224-to-ts.test.ts bun test currently hits an unrelated timeout in browser bundle websafe guard
body:
## Summary
- convert EasyEDA `CIRCLE` shapes on silkscreen layers into `pcb_silkscreen_path` output
- approximate silkscreen circles with 24 segments so they render in soup and generated TypeScript footprints
- update affected conversion tests and snapshots to cover the newly emitted silkscreen geometry

## Why
Silkscreen circles were being dropped during conversion. That meant circular outline and marker graphics present in EasyEDA footprints did not make it into the generated soup/TypeScript output or snapshots.

## Impact
Generated footprints now preserve circular silkscreen details more faithfully, improving visual accuracy for downstream rendering and snapshot-based regression coverage.

## Root Cause
The converter handled paths and arcs for silkscreen output, but it did not have a `CIRCLE` branch for silkscreen-layer shapes.

## Validation
- `bun test --timeout 20000 tests/convert-to-soup-tests/C265111.test.ts tests/convert-to-soup-tests/c3178291-to-soup.test.ts tests/convert-to-soup-tests/c46497.test.ts tests/convert-to-soup-tests/c88224.test.ts tests/convert-to-soup-tests/esp32-to-soup.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C113367-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C12084-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C128415-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C19076967-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C2040-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C23689428-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C265111-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C2652953-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C2838502-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C2848306-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C2913206-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C2998002-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C3178291-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C393941-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C46497-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C51950748-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C7203002-to-ts.test.ts tests/convert-to-ts/C88224-to-ts.test.ts`
- `bun test` currently hits an unrelated timeout in `browser bundle websafe guard`

title: add C51950748 test snapshots
description: Add fixture-backed coverage for C51950748 in the convert-to-ts suite, including test assets and snapshots for 3D rendering and PCB SVG output.
body:
## Summary
Add fixture-backed coverage for `C51950748` in the `convert-to-ts` suite.

## What changed
- added `tests/assets/C51950748.raweasy.json`
- added `tests/convert-to-ts/C51950748-to-ts.test.ts`
- added the 3D snapshot for the generated TSX output
- added the PCB SVG snapshot with `showCourtyards: true`
- set an explicit per-test timeout because this module's 3D render takes longer than Bun's default 5s timeout

## Why
`C51950748` did not have regression coverage. This adds both codegen and rendered snapshot coverage so future converter changes are less likely to break this ESP32-C5 module silently.

## Impact
This expands snapshot coverage for the converter without changing runtime library behavior.

## Validation
- `bun test tests/convert-to-ts/C51950748-to-ts.test.ts`

title: Fix invalid source trace IDs in region reroute output
description: Fixes invalid source trace IDs in reroute output by ensuring that source_trace_id is only assigned when it resolves to exactly one real source trace.
body:
Parses reroute output source trace references from _reroute_ and merged __ connection
  names, then only assigns pcb_trace.source_trace_id when it resolves to exactly one real
  source trace. Reuses the parser for existing trace replacement cleanup and updates the
  Arduino Uno invalid-source-id repro to assert the fixed invariant.

title: Fix Arduino Uno region reroute cleanup
description: Refactors the Arduino Uno reroute repro helpers, adds SVG-backed repro coverage for multiple reroute regions, and fixes stale imported trace geometry left behind after merged region reroutes by expanding merged _reroute_ connection names back to all original source trace IDs before deletion.
body:
Refactors the Arduino Uno reroute repro helpers, adds SVG-backed repro coverage for
  multiple reroute regions, and fixes stale imported trace geometry left behind after merged
  region reroutes by expanding merged _reroute_ connection names back to all original source
  trace IDs before deletion.

title: fix knockout text anchor alignment
description: Fixes knockout text placement so anchor_alignment is honored for knockout-rendered text in both silkscreen and copper rendering.
body:
## Summary

This fixes knockout text placement so `anchor_alignment` is honored for knockout-rendered text in both silkscreen and copper rendering.

## What Changed

- added a shared `getCenteredTextAnchorOffset(...)` helper for `NinePointAnchor`
- applied that offset in knockout silkscreen rendering
- applied that offset in knockout copper rendering
- added regression tests and snapshots for non-center knockout anchors on top and bottom layers

## Root Cause

Knockout text rendering was treating `anchor_position` as if it were always the text center.

That happened because the knockout path generated centered glyph geometry and then rendered it directly at `anchor_position`, but unlike the regular text path it never adjusted for `anchor_alignment`.

As a result:

- `center` anchors rendered correctly
- non-center anchors like `top_left`, `bottom_left`, and `bottom_right` were misplaced
- downstream converters had to compensate upstream by pre-centering the anchor, which is the wrong layer to fix this

## Why Previous Snapshots Did Not Change

Most existing knockout snapshots were already using `anchor_alignment: "center"`.

Since the bug only affected non-center anchors, those snapshots were already exercising the one case that happened to work. That is why the root-cause fix did not materially change the old knockout snapshots.

The new tests cover the missing cases explicitly:

- knockout silkscreen text with `top_left`, `bottom_right`, and bottom-layer `bottom_left`
- knockout copper text with the same non-center alignments

## Impact

- downstream tools can preserve original anchor semantics instead of baking in renderer-specific workarounds
- KiCad-derived knockout text now lines up correctly when the source anchor is not centered
- the fix applies consistently across both silkscreen and copper knockout rendering

## Validation

- `bun test tests/pcb/pcb-silkscreen-text-knockout-anchor-alignment.test.ts tests/pcb/pcb-copper-text-knockout-anchor-alignment.test.ts`
- `bun test tests/pcb/pcb-silkscreen-text-knockout4.test.ts tests/pcb/pcb-copper-text.test.ts`
- validated downstream against the Arduino Nano repro in `kicad-to-circuit-json`

title: Handle through-pad PCB trace segments in SVG rendering and bounds
description: Fixes pcb_trace.route handling for the new through_pad route-point variant from circuit-json, ensuring correct rendering and bounds calculation for through-pad traces in SVG output.
body:
This PR fixes pcb_trace.route handling for the new through_pad route-point variant from circuit-json.

  Changes:

  - normalize PCB trace geometry so through_pad segments are interpreted correctly
  - render through_pad traces across their relevant PCB layers in SVG output
  - include through_pad endpoints in PCB bounds calculation
  - add regression tests covering both bounds and SVG rendering
  - add a snapshot test for a trace that transitions through a pad

  Why this is strong against the guide:

  - this is a bug fix with reproduction in the same PR via tests
  - it touches SVG generation directly
  - it fixes a subtle rendering/data-interpretation issue rather than just types or dependency churn

title: Fix mounting holes inside own courtyard
description: Fixes duplicate courtyard grouping in the PCB component overlap check, ensuring courtyard elements are added only once to each component and preventing duplicate footprint overlap errors while maintaining existing DRC behavior.
body: Fixes duplicate courtyard grouping in the PCB component overlap check. Courtyard elements are now added to each component only once, preventing duplicate footprint overlap errors while keeping the hole-vs-courtyard DRC behavior unchanged.

title: Detect footprint overlap when holes collides with courtyards
description: Updates the PCB component overlap check to include component courtyards in footprint overlap checks against standalone PCB holes, ensuring DRC reports violations correctly.
body:
This updates the PCB component overlap check so component courtyards are treated as part of the footprint when checking against standalone PCB holes.

Previously, a <hole /> could overlap a component’s courtyard without producing the existing pcb_footprint_overlap_error. The check now includes courtyard rect, circle, outline, and polygon elements in the component overlap set, while still skipping non-hole courtyard comparisons so the existing courtyard-overlap check remains responsible for courtyard-vs-courtyard cases.

Motivation
A component courtyard represents occupied footprint space. If a standalone hole intersects that footprint area, the DRC should report it using the existing footprint overlap error instead of missing the violation or introducing a separate hole-specific error.

title: Fix blind vias being emitted in through-board drill output
description: Fixes drill generation for boards with blind vias by separating drill outputs for blind and through vias, ensuring accurate drill files are created.
body:
This fixes drill generation for boards with blind vias.

  Before this change, a blind via from top -> inner1 was being written into the same drill file as a normal through via from top -> bottom. That made the bottom drill snapshot show a drill hole for a via that does not actually reach the bottom layer.

  Now drill output is split by the layers each plated drill connects:

  - drill-L1-L2.drl contains blind vias from layer 1 to layer 2
  - drill-L1-L4.drl contains through vias from layer 1 to layer 4
  - drill_npth.drl still contains non-plated holes

  The repro test now checks that the blind via is not included in the through-board drill output, and adds snapshots showing the separate drill files.

title: Repro: Blind via drill emitted on bottom layer without copper pad
description: Reproduces a bug where a blind via drill is incorrectly emitted on the bottom layer without a copper pad in the Gerber output.

title: use resolveAndLoadKicad3dModelFiles for Kicad 3d models in tscircuit.com
description: Updates the KiCad project build path to use resolveAndLoadKicad3dModelFiles from circuit-json-to-kicad0.0.137, ensuring 3D model assets are included in project exports for a self-contained ZIP file.
body:
Updates the KiCad project build path to use resolveAndLoadKicad3dModelFiles from circuit-json-to-kicad@0.0.137. This removes duplicated 3D model path logic from the CLI and keeps model file placement aligned with the KiCad PCB paths generated by the converter.

Motivation:
KiCad project exports currently include the board files but not the 3D model assets, so models can appear missing when the ZIP is opened elsewhere. Packaging the models with the project makes the export self-contained and closer to the CLI behavior.

title: Add unpkg fallback for worker CDN fetch
description: Add a fallback mechanism for loading webworker entrypoints from an alternative CDN when the primary jsDelivr URL fails or returns a non-OK response.
body:
### Motivation
- Ensure loading the default webworker entrypoint is resilient by trying an alternate CDN when the primary jsDelivr URL fails or returns a non-OK response.

### Description
- Add `getWebWorkerEntrypointCdnUrls(evalVersion)` which returns the jsDelivr and `unpkg` entrypoint URLs for a given `eval` version in `lib/worker.ts`.
- Add `fetchWebWorkerEntrypointBlobFromCdn(evalVersion, verbose)` which iterates the CDN URLs, attempts to `fetch` each, logs warnings on failures, and returns the first successful `Blob` or throws the last error.
- Update `createCircuitWebWorker` to call `fetchWebWorkerEntrypointBlobFromCdn` when `webWorkerBlobUrl` is not provided so the worker is loaded from CDN fallbacks.
- Add a regression test `tests/features/webworker-cdn-fallback.test.ts` that simulates jsDelivr returning 404 and verifies a subsequent `unpkg` fetch is attempted and its blob is returned.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/features/webworker-cdn-fallback.test.ts`, which passed (1 test, 1 pass).
- Ran `bun run format:check` (biome format) which reported no fixes needed.
- The new test verifies the requested URLs sequence matches `getWebWorkerEntrypointCdnUrls("1.2.3")` and that the returned blob content is correct.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_6a037ebefa5883278ccc4b5b2a63136f)

title: Support all PCB fabrication note elements in footprint conversion
description: Expands circuit-json to tscircuit footprint generation to support the full PCB fabrication note family, including text, rectangles, and dimensions with preserved attributes.
body:
## Summary

This PR expands `circuit-json` -> `tscircuit` footprint generation to support the full PCB fabrication note family.

## Changes

- add support for `pcb_fabrication_note_text`
- add support for `pcb_fabrication_note_rect`
- add support for `pcb_fabrication_note_dimension`
- preserve additional fabrication path attributes like `layer` and `color`
- preserve fabrication rect attributes like `corner_radius`, stroke, fill, and dashed state
- preserve fabrication dimension attributes like `text`, `font`, `font_size`, `arrow_size`, `offset`, and `color`
- update the KiCad example snapshot to include generated fabrication text output
- add a regression test covering fabrication path/text/rect/dimension round-trip behavior

title: Fix blank preview in keyboard tutorial
description: Fixes blank preview in the Building a Keyboard with tscircuit tutorial by disabling routing for the final 60 keyboard example.
body: This pr fixes the blank preview in the Building a Keyboard with tscircuit tutorial by disabling routing for the final 60% keyboard example. The smaller 4-key keyboard preview can render with routing enabled, but the 60% layout creates a much larger matrix and can fail or time out during embedded preview rendering. The overview example on the same page already uses `routingDisabled`; this makes the final example consistent with it. Tested locally. ### Before - ### After -

body: ### Reason for model's in the test snapshot loosing color Now the geometry is stored once and each component placement references it. That reduces file size, but `occt-import-js` (the Package is not maintained) does not propagate the original face-level STYLED_ITEM colors through those mapped instances . #### Verified in the viewer that the actual model retains the color

title: Parse solid fills for graphics
description: Parses fill solid as a filled shape for fp_rect, fp_circle, gr_rect, and board gr_poly, ensuring filled graphics are correctly represented and preventing fill registration collisions.
body:
## Summary
- parse `fill solid` as a filled shape for `fp_rect`, `fp_circle`, `gr_rect`, and board `gr_poly`
- add focused regressions covering those parser paths
- prevent pad-primitive `gr_poly` fill registration from overriding the board-level `gr_poly` fill parser

## Why
KiCad emits `fill solid` for filled graphic shapes, but these classes only treated `fill yes` as truthy. That caused filled graphics to be parsed as unfilled.

There was also a registry collision between board `gr_poly` fill parsing and pad-primitive `gr_poly` fill parsing, which meant the board parser could still resolve through the wrong fill class.

## Impact
Filled KiCad graphics now preserve their fill state when parsed, including silkscreen artwork that relies on `fill solid`.

## Validation
- `bun test tests/sexpr/classes/FpRect.test.ts tests/sexpr/classes/FpCircle.test.ts tests/sexpr/classes/GrRect.test.ts tests/sexpr/classes/GrPoly.test.ts tests/sexpr/classes/Footprint.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run build`

title: Add KiCad 3D model file loading helper
description: This adds resolveAndLoadKicad3dModelFiles so circuit-json-to-kicad owns the KiCad 3D model output path convention while callers still control how files are fetched, read, and written. The helper supports builtin model CDN URLs, custom CAD URLs, local model files, and optional best-effort error handling for consumers like the CLI.
body:
This adds resolveAndLoadKicad3dModelFiles so circuit-json-to-kicad owns the KiCad 3D model output path convention while callers still control how files are fetched, read, and written. The helper supports builtin model CDN URLs, custom CAD URLs, local model files, and optional best-effort error handling for consumers like the CLI.

Motivation

CLI, runframe, and the website all need to package the same 3D model files referenced by the generated KiCad PCB. Centralizing the model path resolution avoids each consumer duplicating the same 3dmodels/... folder logic and reduces the chance that exported projects reference missing or incorrectly placed STEP files.

**What Changed**: Copper pours are now emitted with correct geometry (rect, polygon, BRep), mapped to the right net, and use clear default fill settings.

title: preserve board text rotation
description: Preserves standalone board text rotation when collecting KiCad gr_text into Circuit JSON, ensuring rotated labels are correctly represented in the output.
body:
## Summary

This preserves standalone board text rotation when collecting KiCad `gr_text` into Circuit JSON.

## What changed

- capture `at.angle` in `CollectGraphicsStage` and map it to `ccw_rotation`
- keep the value on board-level silkscreen, fabrication-note, and copper text
- add explicit Nano regression assertions for rotated labels like `3.3V`, `D13`, and `GITHUB.COM/SABOGALC`
- refresh the affected Arduino Nano and Arduino Uno snapshots

## Why

Board text rotation was being dropped during KiCad -> Circuit JSON conversion. Rotated labels were emitted with only position, so downstream rendering treated them as unrotated text.

## Impact

- rotated board labels now round-trip correctly through Circuit JSON
- the change is scoped to standalone board text collected from KiCad graphics
- zero-degree text remains effectively unchanged

## Validation

- `bun test tests/repros/arduino-nano/arduino-nano-pcb.test.ts`
- `bun test tests/repros/repro02-arduino-uno/repro02-arduino-uno.test.ts`
- `bun test tests/repros/repro02-arduino-uno/arduino-uno-via-overlay.test.ts`

title: use typed kicadts zones for copper pours
description: Summary This PR fixes copper-pour generation by switching CollectZonesStage to the typed kicadts zone API and bumping kicadts to the release that includes the merged typed-zone work. Concretely, the zone-to-copper-pour path now reads: zone.fill zone.filledPolygons zone.polygons zone.layer  zone.layers filled_polygon layer information pts arc segments via the shared arc approximation helper and this repo now depends on kicadts 0.0.37 instead of 0.0.33.  Root Cause There were two separate mismatches: 1. CollectZonesStage in this repo was still manually reparsing zone._rawChildren instead of using the parsed Zone API. 2. The PR branch was still committed with kicadts 0.0.33, while the typed zone fields used by the downstream change are provided by the newer merged kicadts release. That combination caused copper pours to disappear and led to runtime failures like: TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating zone.filledPolygons.length) in CI when the older dependency version was installed.  What Changed remove manual _rawChildren parsing from CollectZonesStage detect filled zones from typed zone.fill and zone.filledPolygons emit copper pours from typed filledPolygons first, then fall back to typed polygons resolve copper-pour layers from zone.layer, zone.layers, and per-filled_polygon layer data expand pts arc segments with the existing shared arc helper so polygon point extraction stays aligned with the rest of the PCB pipeline bump kicadts from 0.0.33 to 0.0.37 update the affected snapshots for via_grid_template and repro01-joule-thief  Impact This keeps KiCad parsing responsibility in kicadts and removes the repo-local zone parser from kicad-to-circuit-json. It also restores copper pours for the fixtures that depend on zone output, including multi-layer zone cases such as layers F.Cu B.Cu, and makes CI install the kicadts version that actually exposes the typed zone fields used by this change.  Validation bunx tsc --noEmit bun test .testsreprosrepro02-arduino-unoarduino-uno-trace-grouping.test.ts bun test .testsvia-grid-template.test.ts bun test .testsreprosrepro01-joule-thiefrepro01-joule-thief-pcb.test.ts
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes copper-pour generation by switching `CollectZonesStage` to the typed `kicadts` zone API and bumping `kicadts` to the release that includes the merged typed-zone work.

Concretely, the zone-to-copper-pour path now reads:

- `zone.fill`
- `zone.filledPolygons`
- `zone.polygons`
- `zone.layer` / `zone.layers`
- `filled_polygon` layer information
- `pts` arc segments via the shared arc approximation helper

and this repo now depends on `kicadts ^0.0.37` instead of `^0.0.33`.

## Root Cause

There were two separate mismatches:

1. `CollectZonesStage` in this repo was still manually reparsing `zone._rawChildren` instead of using the parsed `Zone` API.
2. The PR branch was still committed with `kicadts ^0.0.33`, while the typed zone fields used by the downstream change are provided by the newer merged `kicadts` release.

That combination caused copper pours to disappear and led to runtime failures like:

- `TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'zone.filledPolygons.length')`

in CI when the older dependency version was installed.

## What Changed

- remove manual `_rawChildren` parsing from `CollectZonesStage`
- detect filled zones from typed `zone.fill` and `zone.filledPolygons`
- emit copper pours from typed `filledPolygons` first, then fall back to typed `polygons`
- resolve copper-pour layers from `zone.layer`, `zone.layers`, and per-`filled_polygon` layer data
- expand `pts` arc segments with the existing shared arc helper so polygon point extraction stays aligned with the rest of the PCB pipeline
- bump `kicadts` from `^0.0.33` to `^0.0.37`
- update the affected snapshots for `via_grid_template` and `repro01-joule-thief`

## Impact

This keeps KiCad parsing responsibility in `kicadts` and removes the repo-local zone parser from `kicad-to-circuit-json`.

It also restores copper pours for the fixtures that depend on zone output, including multi-layer zone cases such as `layers "F.Cu" "B.Cu"`, and makes CI install the `kicadts` version that actually exposes the typed zone fields used by this change.

## Validation

- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun test ./tests/repros/repro02-arduino-uno/arduino-uno-trace-grouping.test.ts`
- `bun test ./tests/via-grid-template.test.ts`
- `bun test ./tests/repros/repro01-joule-thief/repro01-joule-thief-pcb.test.ts`

title: Preserve KiCad knockout board text in Circuit JSON
description: Summary Preserve KiCad board-level gr_text knockout semantics when converting silkscreen text into Circuit JSON.  What changed detect KiCad knockout on board silkscreen gr_text in CollectGraphicsStage emit pcb_silkscreen_text.is_knockout  true for those items add targeted Arduino Nano repro assertions for the board texts NANO and GND update the Arduino Nano PNG golden to reflect the rendered knockout behavior  Why Board-level gr_text items with KiCad knockout were being emitted as ordinary pcb_silkscreen_text entries, so the knockout intent was lost in Circuit JSON and in downstream rendering.  Impact preserves silkscreen knockout semantics for board text imports tightens regression coverage around the existing Arduino Nano repro keeps the change scoped to the board text conversion path  Root cause CollectGraphicsStage converted board gr_text into pcb_silkscreen_text without checking whether the KiCad layer metadata included knockout.  Validation bunx tsc --noEmit bun test testsreprosarduino-nanoarduino-nano-pcb.test.ts
body:
## Summary

Preserve KiCad board-level `gr_text` knockout semantics when converting silkscreen text into Circuit JSON.

## What changed

- detect KiCad `knockout` on board silkscreen `gr_text` in `CollectGraphicsStage`
- emit `pcb_silkscreen_text.is_knockout = true` for those items
- add targeted Arduino Nano repro assertions for the board texts `NANO` and `GND`
- update the Arduino Nano PNG golden to reflect the rendered knockout behavior

## Why

Board-level `gr_text` items with KiCad `knockout` were being emitted as ordinary `pcb_silkscreen_text` entries, so the knockout intent was lost in Circuit JSON and in downstream rendering.

## Impact

- preserves silkscreen knockout semantics for board text imports
- tightens regression coverage around the existing Arduino Nano repro
- keeps the change scoped to the board text conversion path

## Root cause

`CollectGraphicsStage` converted board `gr_text` into `pcb_silkscreen_text` without checking whether the KiCad layer metadata included `knockout`.

## Validation

- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun test tests/repros/arduino-nano/arduino-nano-pcb.test.ts`

title: Fix KRT rerouting when endpoints sit on reroute region bounds
description: This fixes a KRT rerouting failure where the router reported no route for a valid reroute segment whose endpoints were exactly on the reroute region boundary. The issue showed up from cores autoroutingphase reroute  flow. Core was passing a valid SimpleRouteJson: the reroute region was bounded, and the segment endpoints were placed on the clipped region edges. KRT failed because its clearance-margin check treated cells near those boundary endpoints like normal routing cells. Since the required clearance margin extended outside the reroute bounds, the router rejected the endpoint area and returned no route. The fix updates GridObstacleMap::is_blocked_with_margin to respect existing endpoint exemptions. Cells within the endpoint exemption radius now skip the normal margin expansion and only check whether the cell itself is blocked. Normal routing cells still use the full clearance-margin behavior. Also adds a visual snapshot test that reproduces the core-style reroute case with a subcircuit and autoroutingphase reroute .
body:
This fixes a KRT rerouting failure where the router reported no route for a valid reroute segment whose endpoints were exactly on the reroute region boundary.

  The issue showed up from core’s <autoroutingphase reroute /> flow. Core was passing a valid SimpleRouteJson: the reroute region was bounded, and the segment endpoints were placed on the clipped region edges. KRT failed because its clearance-margin check treated cells near those boundary endpoints like normal routing cells. Since the required clearance margin extended outside the reroute bounds, the router rejected the endpoint area and returned “no route”.

  The fix updates GridObstacleMap::is_blocked_with_margin to respect existing endpoint exemptions. Cells within the endpoint exemption radius now skip the normal margin expansion and only check whether the cell itself is blocked. Normal routing cells still use the full clearance-margin behavior.

  This is a router-level fix rather than a core workaround because endpoints are valid special cases: the router must be able to start or finish on pads, existing traces, or clipped reroute boundaries even when normal clearance expansion would reject nearby space.

  Also adds a visual snapshot test that reproduces the core-style reroute case with a subcircuit and <autoroutingphase reroute />.

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.519
description: Automated package update

title: Fix reroute reachability for overlapping target nodes
description: Fixes reroute reachability for overlapping same-layer target nodes in the capacity mesh, ensuring reroute endpoints embedded in padtarget copper are reachable by the tiny-hypergraph pathing stage.
body:
Connect overlapping same-layer target nodes in the capacity mesh so reroute endpoints
  embedded in pad/target copper are reachable by the tiny-hypergraph pathing stage.

  Preserve existing rootConnectionName through point-pair expansion and connection merging
  so reroute segments keep their original net identity.

title: fix: stitch solver disconnecting the bridge fragment of the same root net
description: Fixes the issue where the stitch solver disconnects the bridge fragment of the same root net during autorouting.
body: ## Before ## After

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.517
description: Automated package update

title: Fix same-root MST route disconnections in repro117
description: Repairs disconnected intra-node route fragments when a same-root MST sibling already contains the missing local bridge, preserving connectivity for cases like cmn_68 in repro117.
body:
Repairs disconnected intra-node route fragments when a same-root MST sibling already
  contains the missing local bridge, preserving connectivity for cases like cmn_68 in
  repro117.

title: Implement Automatic LightBurn Project Splitting by Cut Setting with Group-Aware Shape Isolation
description: Adds a new split-by-cut-setting utility that parses LightBurn project XML and generates separate .lbrn2 outputs for each cut setting, preserving relevant project metadata, filtering shapes by cut index, handling nested groups, producing safe unique filenames, and reporting shape counts per generated file. Also exports the new API from both root and lib entrypoints and adds a real-world LightBurn fixture for coverage.
body:
Adds a new split-by-cut-setting utility that parses LightBurn project XML and generates separate .lbrn2 outputs for each cut setting, preserving relevant project metadata, filtering shapes by cut index, handling nested groups, producing safe unique filenames, and reporting shape counts per generated file.

Also exports the new API from both root and lib entrypoints and adds a real-world LightBurn fixture for coverage.

title: Add benchmark summary table for samples with remaining DRC errors
description: Adds a summary table to log samples that have remaining DRC errors after benchmarking, displaying sample number, ID, DRC counts, iterations, and elapsed time.

title: Add interactive DRC visualization tooling with selectable error markers and layer-aware graphics
description: Add interactive DRC visualization tooling that allows users to visualize design rule check (DRC) errors with selectable error markers and layer-aware graphics, enhancing the debugging process for circuit designs.

title: Introduce Bun-Powered GitHub Actions for Automated Format and Type Validation
description: Adds CI workflows for automated formatting and TypeScript validation using Bun and GitHub Actions, ensuring code quality on every push and pull request to the main branch.
body: Adds CI workflows for automated formatting and TypeScript validation using Bun and GitHub Actions. The new pipelines install dependencies, run Biome format checks, and enforce type safety on every push and pull request targeting main.

title: Introduce React Cosmos + Vite Frontend Workspace with Tailwind and TypeScript Tooling
description: Sets up a new frontend workspace using React Cosmos, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript for improved development and styling capabilities.

title: init: added License and biome.json
description: Adds an MIT License file and a biome.json configuration file for code formatting and linting.

title: Improve Global DRC route translation, broad-force recovery, and endpoint-connected relaxation
description: Enhances the Global DRC solver by improving route translation, broad-force recovery, and endpoint connection handling for better routing efficiency.

title: Introduce Interactive PCB Fabrication Workflow Workspace with Deep-Linked Job Execution Stages
description: Adds an interactive PCB fabrication workflow with deep-linking for job execution stages, allowing users to navigate through different fabrication stages directly from the dashboard.

title: added Anas as codeowner
description: Adds Anas as a code owner for the repository, indicating responsibility for code review and maintenance.

title: Added build check workflow
description: Adds a GitHub Actions workflow for building the project on push and pull request events to the main branch.

title: Introduce Live PCB SVG Rendering for Fabrication Job Previews
description: Replaces static mock PCB illustrations with dynamically rendered PCB previews generated directly from real circuit JSON assets using circuit-to-svg.
body: Replaces static mock PCB illustrations with dynamically rendered PCB previews generated directly from real circuit JSON assets using circuit-to-svg.

title: Introduce PCBBurn Fabrication Dashboard UI with Job Queue, Upload Flow, and Workspace Layout
description: Adds a new dashboard UI for managing PCB fabrication jobs, including job queue, upload functionality, and workspace layout.
